posters.title: AsymLoc: Towards Asymmetric Feature Matching for Efficient Visual Localization
posters.abstract: Visual localization — estimating camera pose from a query image against a reference database — traditionally relies on symmetric feature descriptors that treat query and database images identically. We show this symmetry is computationally wasteful: query images benefit from compact, discriminative representations while the reference database benefits from richer, higher-dimensional descriptors that support precise matching. AsymLoc designs these two descriptor streams independently, applying aggressive compression to query features and richer processing to database entries. This asymmetry achieves accuracy on par with symmetric approaches while significantly reducing retrieval latency and memory footprint, enabling efficient large-scale visual localization.

posters.title: CodeV: Code with Images for Faithful Visual Reasoning via Tool-Aware Policy Optimization
posters.abstract: Visual reasoning in large language models is hampered by a tendency to generate plausible-sounding but factually incorrect descriptions of image content. CodeV augments visual reasoning by generating executable code that operates on images through specialized tools — segmenters, depth estimators, measurement routines — rather than relying solely on direct visual description. We train a policy via reinforcement learning that learns when and how to invoke image-processing tools and integrate their outputs into multi-step reasoning chains. Tool-aware policy optimization substantially reduces hallucination and improves faithfulness on spatially demanding visual question-answering benchmarks.

posters.title: FlowFixer: Towards Detail-Preserving Subject-Driven Generation
posters.abstract: Subject-driven image generation — synthesizing novel scenes that faithfully reproduce a specified person or object — often sacrifices fine-grained identity detail in favor of scene coherence. FlowFixer addresses this with a rectified flow backbone and a dedicated detail-preservation branch that disentangles subject identity from scene composition. A flow-guided attention mechanism anchors subject details across diverse scene conditions by grounding generation in high-frequency identity features extracted from reference images. FlowFixer achieves state-of-the-art fidelity on subject identity preservation while producing coherent, high-quality scenes across a wide range of text prompts.

posters.title: ImageRAGTurbo: Towards One-step Text-to-Image Generation with Retrieval-Augmented Diffusion Models
posters.abstract: We propose ImageRAGTurbo, a one-step text-to-image generation method that combines retrieval-augmented generation with diffusion models. By retrieving relevant images at inference time and conditioning the diffusion process on them, we achieve high-quality synthesis in a single forward pass, dramatically reducing latency while preserving semantic fidelity.

posters.title: MM-ReCoder: Advancing Chart-to-Code Generation with Reinforcement Learning and Self-Correction
posters.abstract: Chart-to-code generation — recovering executable visualization code from a rendered chart image — is essential for chart editing and accessibility, yet existing methods struggle with complex layouts and precise value recovery. MM-ReCoder frames the task as a sequence generation problem and applies reinforcement learning with execution-based rewards to drive code correctness. A self-correction module iteratively refines generated code by detecting and fixing semantic errors through multiple edit cycles, using the rendered output as a visual feedback signal. MM-ReCoder significantly outperforms prior methods on chart reconstruction fidelity and code executability across diverse chart types and visualization libraries.

posters.title: Reinforcing Structured Chain-of-Thought for Video Understanding
posters.abstract: We introduce a reinforcement learning framework that trains video understanding models to produce structured chain-of-thought reasoning. By explicitly rewarding coherent temporal and causal reasoning chains, our approach significantly improves performance on complex video QA benchmarks while making model decisions more interpretable.

posters.title: Revisiting Model Stitching in the Foundation Model Era
posters.abstract: We revisit model stitching — combining intermediate representations from separately trained networks — in the context of large foundation models. We show that modern foundation models enable much more effective stitching than previously possible, allowing practitioners to mix encoder and decoder components across architectures and modalities with minimal fine-tuning.

posters.title: RMIR: A Benchmark Dataset for Reasoning-Intensive Multimodal Image Retrieval
posters.abstract: We introduce RMIR, a new benchmark for multimodal image retrieval that specifically targets queries requiring complex multi-step reasoning over both image content and textual descriptions. Unlike existing retrieval benchmarks, RMIR requires models to perform relational, compositional, and causal reasoning to identify correct matches, exposing significant gaps in current vision-language retrieval systems.

posters.title: S2D: Selective Spectral Decay for Quantization-Friendly Conditioning of Neural Activations
posters.abstract: Post-training quantization of neural networks often degrades performance because activation distributions vary widely across layers, making them difficult to represent at low bit-widths. We propose S2D (Selective Spectral Decay), a training-time regularization technique that conditions neural activations to have quantization-friendly spectral properties. By selectively penalizing high-frequency spectral components in activation tensors, S2D produces smooth, well-behaved distributions that quantize with minimal accuracy loss. S2D is architecture-agnostic and imposes negligible training overhead while enabling effective 4-bit and 8-bit quantization across vision transformer and CNN architectures.

posters.title: Universal Guideline-Driven Image Clustering via a Hybrid LLM Agent
posters.abstract: We present a universal image clustering framework driven by natural-language guidelines and powered by a hybrid LLM agent. Rather than optimising a fixed objective, our system accepts arbitrary user-defined criteria (e.g., cluster by depicted activity vs. by scene type) and uses LLM-guided proposal and refinement to produce guideline-compliant clusters without retraining.

posters.title: Visual Grounding for Object Questions
posters.abstract: We address visual grounding for object questions — the task of precisely localising image regions that answer natural-language questions about objects. We introduce a two-stage approach that first identifies candidate objects via language-driven attention, then refines bounding-box predictions using cross-modal reasoning, achieving state-of-the-art grounding accuracy on multiple benchmarks.

posters.title: Visual Reasoning through Tool-supervised Reinforcement Learning
posters.abstract: Large vision-language models often fail at tasks requiring precise spatial reasoning, counting, or geometric operations — capabilities better handled by specialized visual tools than by direct generative inference. We propose a reinforcement learning framework that trains VLMs to invoke external visual tools (depth estimators, segmenters, OCR) when beneficial, using tool-execution outcomes as reward signals. The framework learns a policy for when to invoke tools and how to integrate their outputs into multi-step reasoning chains, achieving significant gains on compositional visual reasoning benchmarks without task-specific supervision.

posters.title: δYNAMICS: Language-based Representation for Inferring Rigid-body Dynamics from Videos
posters.abstract: Inferring rigid-body dynamics — forces, masses, and motion trajectories — directly from video is a key step toward physical world models, but is ill-posed without explicit physical parameterization. δYNAMICS introduces language-based physical representations that use structured natural-language descriptors to encode physical properties of objects observed in video. These representations are grounded in both visual observations and physics priors, enabling a model to infer dynamics parameters from a few frames and predict future rigid-body motion. Evaluations on synthetic and real-world physics benchmarks demonstrate superior prediction accuracy compared to purely visual or purely physics-based baselines.

oral.title: Lafite: A Generative Latent Field for 3D Native Texturing
oral.abstract: Texturing 3D objects is fundamentally constrained by the need for UV unwrapping or multi-view projection — both of which introduce seams, distortions, and limited generalization. Lafite approaches 3D texturing natively, learning a generative latent field defined directly in 3D space. We represent texture and geometry jointly using a local vector field with a structured latent code, enabling a model that understands the 3D structure rather than projecting 2D cues. The result is a texture generator that avoids seams entirely, handles complex topologies naturally, and supports downstream tasks including semantic editing and inpainting. Lafite achieves state-of-the-art on standard 3D texture benchmarks and was recognized as a CVPR 2026 Highlight paper.
oral.session: Saturday, June 6, 2026 | 10:45 AM – 12:45 PM PDT | ExHall F 533

booth.tagline: Building the future of computer vision at scale
booth.description: Amazon's computer vision teams power Rekognition (face analysis, object detection, scene understanding), Just Walk Out Technology in Amazon Go stores, Alexa Vision, and fulfillment center automation. We operate at a scale few companies can match — billions of images analyzed daily — and publish extensively at top venues including CVPR, ICCV, and NeurIPS.
openRoles: ML Engineer – Just Walk Out Technology
